name: Barbell Back Squat
instructions: Unrack the barbell so it rests securely across your upper traps or rear delts.
Step back and set your feet roughly shoulder-width apart, toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core, take a deep breath, and initiate the movement by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Lower yourself until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor.
Drive through your mid-foot to stand back up to the starting position.
tips: Keep your chest up and a neutral spine throughout the movement. Do not let your knees cave inward as you push up.

name: Standard Push-Up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands placed slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your body in a straight line from your head to your heels.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your chest is just above the floor.
Push back up to the starting position by fully extending your arms.
tips: Keep your elbows tucked at a 45-degree angle to your body to protect your shoulders. Do not let your lower back sag.

name: Conventional Deadlift
instructions: Stand with your mid-foot under the barbell, feet hip-width apart.
Hinge at your hips and grip the bar just outside your knees.
Drop your hips slightly, lift your chest, and flatten your back.
Take a deep breath, brace your core, and drive through the floor with your legs to lift the weight.
Stand tall at the top with your hips fully extended, then hinge at the hips to return the bar to the floor.
tips: Keep the barbell close to your body (dragging up your shins). Avoid rounding your lower back at any point during the lift.

name: Pull-Up
instructions: Grab the pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Hang freely with your arms fully extended (dead hang).
Pull your shoulder blades down and back, then pull your chest up to the bar.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower yourself slowly and with control back to the starting position.
tips: Focus on driving your elbows down to the floor rather than just pulling with your biceps. Minimize swinging (kipping) to maximize muscle engagement.

name: Dumbbell Overhead Press (Seated)
instructions: Sit on a bench with back support, holding a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
Kick the dumbbells up to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at your shoulders.
tips: Keep your core tight and avoid excessively arching your lower back. Don't let the dumbbells clank together at the top.
